prompt:
A Community Commerce & Content Operations Management System that includes recipe publishing, offline business analytics, and settlement reporting for a multi-site organization running local community sales programs. Users are divided into Administrator, Content Editor, Reviewer, Operations Analyst, Finance Clerk, and Read-Only Auditor roles. In the Layui-based web interface, Editors create and maintain recipe content with structured steps, quantities, prep/cook time, difficulty levels, tags, and embedded images, with a rich-text editor that supports paste-cleanup and local image upload with preview and size warnings. Recipes move through Draft, In Review, Approved, or Rejected states, and users receive immediate on-screen feedback for missing required fields, invalid units, or oversized files. Reviewers compare versions, add inline comments, and either approve or return content for revision; only Approved items can be published. Operations staff use interactive dashboards to monitor sales, group conversion rate, average order value, repeat purchase rate, refund rate, group leader performance, and product popularity, with filters by date range, community, group leader, and product, and a consistent "metric definition" drawer to prevent misunderstandings. Analysts can run multi-dimensional searches and full-text queries across records (such as positions, companies, participants, and workflow status) and generate configurable reports like participation rate, retention, pass rate, and regional distribution, with one-click export to CSV for offline sharing. Finance users manage charges and settlements by defining freight pricing rules and generating statements, then reconciling variances and routing settlements for approval with a clear audit trail.

The backend uses ThinkPHP to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Layui frontend over the local network, with MySQL as the system of record for recipes, versions, review actions, analytics snapshots, settlements, and audit logs. Recipe modeling enforces validation rules such as step count between 1 and 50, total time between 1 and 720 minutes, and image uploads limited to JPG/PNG under 5 MB; files are stored on local disk with SHA-256 fingerprints to detect duplicates and tampering. Versioning keeps every revision immutable after approval, while drafts can be edited; publishing requires at least one Reviewer approval and records reviewer ID and timestamp. Analytics are calculated from local transactional data using nightly jobs (default 2:00 AM) and can also be refreshed on demand with rate limits of 5 refreshes per user per hour; scheduled report generation supports daily/weekly/monthly runs stored locally for 180 days. The settlement engine supports freight calculations using distance bands entered manually by staff (no maps), weight/volume tiers, per-order surcharges, and sales tax fields; statements lock after approval and any correction requires a reversal entry to preserve accountability. Security is fully offline with username/password login, salted password hashing, role-based permission isolation down to report and export scope, field masking for sensitive identifiers, AES encryption for stored tax IDs where applicable, and immutable operation logs for exports, approvals, and permission changes retained for at least 7 years.

stack: Frontend

Framework: Layui (Modular web UI)

Editor: Custom Rich-Text Editor (Paste-cleanup, local image upload with size warnings)

UI Components: Version comparison view, "Metric Definition" drawers, and interactive data dashboards.

Backend

Language & Framework: PHP with ThinkPHP (RESTful API)

Environment: Fully offline local network deployment.

Storage & Persistence

Database: MySQL (System of record for recipes, analytics snapshots, settlements, and logs)

File Storage: Local disk storage with SHA-256 fingerprinting for duplicate/tamper detection.

Retention: 180-day storage for scheduled reports; 7-year retention for immutable operation logs.

Core Logic & Engines

Content Lifecycle: State-machine workflow (Draft → Review → Approved/Rejected) with immutable versioning.

Analytics Engine: Local transactional data processing via nightly jobs (2:00 AM) or on-demand refresh (Rate-limited).

Settlement Engine: Tiered freight calculations (Distance/Weight/Volume), manual distance bands, and reversal-based correction logic.

Report Generator: Multi-dimensional full-text query engine with CSV export capabilities.

Security & Compliance

Auth: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) with granular export/report scope isolation.

Data Protection: Salted password hashing, AES-256 encryption for tax IDs, and sensitive field masking.

Auditability: Immutable audit trails for financial settlements, permission changes, and data exports.

Validation: Strict server-side modeling (e.g., 50-step recipe limits, 5 MB image caps).
